stack.rationale: ISR for storefront SEO, Server Actions for mutations, modern React patterns
Type safety across domain, application, and presentation layers
Matches existing wireframes built with Tailwind, rapid UI development
Type-safe queries, lightweight, good DX with migrations
Relational data model, pgEnum support, production reliability
Shared schemas between client and server, React Hook Form integration
Server state management, caching, real-time updates for admin panel
Simple client-side cart without server sync, lightweight
Next.js native, supports email/password and OAuth providers
Indonesian payment gateway, supports multiple local payment methods
Accessible headless components, composable with Tailwind
Native Next.js support, edge functions, automatic ISR, preview deployments
Reliable transactional email service for OTP and order notifications
Native Vercel integration, simple API for product image uploads

architectureNotes.heading: Vertical Slice Architecture
architectureNotes.body: Each feature is a self-contained module under `features/`. Complex features (products, orders, payment) have full domain/application/presentation layering. Simple features (banners, categories) use presentation-only with direct DB queries. Start simple, add layers only when needed.

architectureNotes.heading: Clean Architecture Layers
architectureNotes.body: Domain layer contains pure TypeScript with zero dependencies. Application layer has direct Drizzle queries (no repository pattern for CRUD). Presentation layer contains UI components and route handlers. No infrastructure abstraction layer.

architectureNotes.heading: Query/Command Split
architectureNotes.body: Commands (mutations) use Server Actions for form submissions. Store queries use direct query import in Server Components (no HTTP overhead). Admin queries use Route Handlers + TanStack Query (HTTP cached, parallelizable).

architectureNotes.heading: Storefront ISR vs Admin CSR
architectureNotes.body: Storefront uses ISR with Server Components for SEO and static HTML generation. Admin uses CSR with TanStack Query for real-time updates. Route handlers serve admin data via HTTP for caching and parallelization.

architectureNotes.heading: Money as Cents
architectureNotes.body: All prices stored as integer cents in database to avoid floating-point issues. Converted to dollars at API boundaries. Display uses formatCurrency helper. DB: priceCents (integer) → API: priceDollars (number) → Display: formatCurrency(cents).

architectureNotes.heading: Payment Gateway Abstraction
architectureNotes.body: PaymentProvider interface allows swapping Midtrans for other providers (Stripe, Xendit) without touching order logic. Webhook handler verifies signature and updates order status atomically.

architectureNotes.heading: Atomic Order Creation
architectureNotes.body: Order + orderItems + payment record inserted in single database transaction. If any fails, all roll back. Idempotency key prevents duplicate orders from double form-submits.

architectureNotes.heading: SEO Implementation
architectureNotes.body: Structured data using JSON-LD for products (schema.org Product, Offer). Dynamic sitemap generated via Next.js sitemap.ts. Per-page meta tags via generateMetadata. Open Graph tags for social sharing. ISR ensures fresh SEO data without rebuilds.

architectureNotes.heading: Image Management
architectureNotes.body: Product images stored in Vercel Blob. Admin uploads via @vercel/blob client. Images served via Vercel's CDN with automatic optimization. No separate CDN service needed.

risks.text: Midtrans sandbox vs production configuration must be clearly separated to avoid payment issues
ISR revalidation must be triggered correctly after product/order mutations to avoid stale storefront data
Cart stored in localStorage only - clearing browser data loses cart contents
No inventory tracking was explicitly dropped - may need to be added later if client requirements change
Overseas shipping calculation adds complexity - need to define supported regions and carrier integrations

openDecisions.text: Shipping calculation: flat rate, weight-based, or carrier API integration? Client wants overseas shipping support - need to define scope and regions
Analytics integration (GA, Hotjar) - deferred but will be needed
